mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat at low levels, discount rates and financing costs stay supportive, so S&P 500 tends to post positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during sustained low-or-declining FEDFUNDS periods, or if the sign relationship reverses in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend tightens real-rate expectations and raises discount-rate pressure on equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above the prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-triggered observations do not show lower subsequent 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if the triggered-hit rate of negative returns falls below 55% over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises alongside slower PAYEMS growth, the labor market is weakening and recession risk is rising, so equity risk premia widen and S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE/PAYEMS deterioration trigger does not produce materially lower 20-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if negative returns occur in less than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth expectations, which supports earnings revisions and lifts S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to coincide with above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year sign accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and better aggregate supply conditions, reducing margin pressure and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO uptrends are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST weakens and GDPC1 decelerates together, housing-sensitive demand is rolling over and forward earnings expectations soften, so S&P 500 should underperform over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average and GDPC1 growth decelerates
failure_condition: Reject if the combined housing-growth slowdown trigger does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns than either input series on its own, or if the triggered sample has a nonnegative median return.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment expansion, falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS indicate broadening labor income, which supports consumption and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with lower UNRATE and rising PAYEMS do not outperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if the sign is negative in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS level above neutral with concurrent CPIAUCSL acceleration implies policy is behind inflation, raising the probability of equity multiple compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation trend is rising
failure_condition: Reject if the tightening-plus-inflation trigger does not produce significantly lower 20-day SPX returns than periods without the trigger, or if positive returns occur in more than 45% of triggered observations.
